- The aggregate group key adds task to the full configuration identity.
- The offline report calculates scorable run count; strict completion count and percentage; partial-credit mean, sample standard deviation, minimum, and maximum; token median, minimum, and maximum; duration median, minimum, and maximum; model-turn and tool-call median and maximum; runs containing tool errors; and termination-reason counts.
- The reporting command writes a deterministic Markdown report and a machine-readable JSON summary.
- The report includes configuration identity, coverage, a panel summary, per-task groups, termination evidence, and references to individual run artifacts.
- Report generation uses no server, database, live UI, JavaScript, charting framework, or subjective model evaluator.
- The existing single-run UI continues to provide detailed execution inspection and is not expanded into an aggregate dashboard.
- Documentation will explain architecture, information flow, evaluation protocol, tradeoffs, run commands, and what would be improved with more time.

## Testing Decisions

- Good tests assert externally visible behavior through stable public seams rather than private planner, executor, or adapter implementation details.
- The primary agent test seam is AgentRuntime.run producing RuntimeOutcome. Scripted model responses and the real AutomationBench adapter/scorer exercise the planner, executor, recovery, context, tool dispatch, tracing, and scoring together.
- The second justified seam is the offline evaluation/report command. Fixture session artifacts exercise resumption, grouping, statistical calculations, coverage checks, and deterministic Markdown and JSON output.
- The evaluator UI remains a consumer of AgentRuntime and does not become a third primary seam.
- Existing prior art includes runtime tests that inject scripted model behavior, execute real benchmark tools, and invoke official scoring; API tests that inject an AgentRuntime; and session-browser tests that materialize persisted artifacts.
- Runtime tests will cover a successful multi-step plan through official scoring.
- Runtime tests will cover planner rejection followed by the one allowed step retry.
- Runtime tests will cover a replan that preserves completed evidence and side effects.
- Runtime tests will cover plan-length, executor-turn, retry, replan, and global-call exhaustion.
- Runtime tests will cover one structured-output correction and repeated protocol failure.
- Runtime tests will cover transient provider retry behavior and distinguish a request defect from an infrastructure-invalid attempt.
- Runtime tests will cover unknown tools, invalid arguments, tool exceptions, and model recovery from structured errors.
- Runtime tests will demonstrate that side-effecting tool calls are not automatically duplicated.
- Runtime tests will confirm accepted-step transcript compression while preserving the immutable trace.
- Runtime tests will confirm retry context retains the previous local attempt and planner feedback.
- Runtime tests will confirm replanning receives completed evidence and a structured failed-step record.
- Runtime tests will capture model-visible requests and confirm evaluator-only fields are absent.
- Runtime tests will verify event ordering, model/step/call correlation, usage accumulation, termination reason, and cancellation.
- Runtime tests will verify that execution completion does not imply strict benchmark completion.
- Batch tests will verify that existing scorable task-repetition pairs are skipped and missing pairs resume.
- Batch tests will verify that infrastructure-invalid attempts do not satisfy a repetition and do not enter aggregate data.
- Report tests will use fixed artifacts with hand-calculated expected statistics.
- Report tests will verify full configuration identity prevents incompatible runs from being combined.
- Report tests will verify deterministic ordering and byte-stable output for unchanged inputs.
- One real development task will be run end to end through the custom runtime, existing UI, persisted artifact, and official scorer before the feature is considered complete.
- Tests will not assert exact private prompt wording, internal helper call sequences, styling, or implementation-specific class decomposition.

## Out of Scope

- A reusable or generalized agent framework.
- A graph or DAG planner.
- Plan-level parallel scheduling.
- Subagents or agent handoffs.
- Multiple model providers or a generalized provider registry.
- Dynamic tool discovery, plugins, or meta-tool search.
- Long-term memory, vector stores, or retrieval over prior runs.
- Human approval or intervention workflows.
- A database, queue, distributed worker system, or concurrent batch evaluation.
- A semantic cache.
- A separate LLM judge or subjective quality score.
- Automatic prompt optimization.
- Statistical hypothesis testing or benchmark-wide claims beyond the selected experiment.
- Token-threshold summarization or arbitrary tool-output truncation in the first version.
- A speculative duplicate-action or stuck-agent classifier beyond explicit budgets.
- A live aggregate dashboard, report server, or charting system.
- Estimated dollar cost without authoritative endpoint pricing.
- Recording the submission video or transforming raw build-session logs.

## Further Notes

- The solution is intentionally narrow: one Sales benchmark domain, one provider endpoint, one planner-executor design, one runtime protocol, and two user-facing evaluation surfaces.
- Strict completion is the primary correctness metric. Partial credit and operational metrics provide diagnosis rather than replacing it.
- Ten repetitions estimate reliability on the selected tasks; the report must retain per-task results and clear coverage.
- The preregistered held-out task manifest is owner-supplied and must not include the four development tasks.
- The final runtime must have no LangChain or LangGraph dependency, even if the exploratory baseline remains documented as historical context.
- Completion requires all scripted tests, one real development execution, unchanged UI interoperability, persisted planner-executor traces, and successful deterministic report generation.
- The implementation should favor small dataclasses, protocols, and plain control flow. New abstraction is justified only when it enforces blindness, preserves the existing runtime seam, or enables scripted testing.

issues.title: Complete harness recovery and replace the framework baseline
issues.body:
## Parent

- #13

## What to build

Complete the custom planner-executor harness by adding its bounded recovery and context behavior, then make it the sole runtime used by the application.

The planner must be able to reject one step attempt, provide corrective feedback, retry it once, and replace an invalid pending plan once while preserving accepted evidence and previous side effects. Every execution must end predictably under the agreed budgets, protocol rules, tool errors, cancellation, or model failures.

Once this behavior is proven through the public runtime seam, remove the exploratory LangChain and LangGraph execution path and dependencies. Preserve existing evaluator UI, durable session, scoring, and trace behavior while extending the trace to explain recovery and terminal states.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A rejected step can be retried once with the prior local transcript and planner feedback available to the executor.
- [ ] A plan can be replaced once while completed steps and accepted evidence remain immutable.
- [ ] Accepted step transcripts are removed from future model-visible context and replaced by structured evidence preserving exact facts, source tool-call IDs, actions, and outcomes.
- [ ] Before replanning, failed attempts become structured records preserving performed actions, side effects that must not be repeated, useful facts, errors, and the invalidation reason.
- [ ] Context is not compressed between ordinary executor turns or before the one permitted retry.
- [ ] Unknown tools, invalid arguments, and tool exceptions are returned as structured correlated errors that the executor can react to.
- [ ] Side-effecting tool calls are never automatically retried by Python.
- [ ] Transient network errors, HTTP 429, and HTTP 5xx receive no more than two provider retries with short backoff.
- [ ] Planner and reviewer schema failures receive one correction attempt and then terminate as model_protocol_error.
- [ ] The runtime enforces no more than six plan steps, four executor model turns per attempt, one step retry, one replan, and 24 logical model calls.
- [ ] Exhausted execution limits terminate as budget_exhausted and remain scorable agent outcomes.
- [ ] Cooperative cancellation produces a stopped outcome at a safe model or completed tool-batch boundary.
- [ ] Retry, replan, protocol correction, cancellation, budget exhaustion, and completion appear as ordered correlated events and durable terminal evidence in the existing UI.
- [ ] High-level scripted tests cover retry, replan, context compression, tool-error recovery, every budget, protocol failure, cancellation, and termination reasons through AgentRuntime.
- [ ] The custom planner-executor becomes the default runtime for the UI and command-line workflow.
- [ ] LangChain and LangGraph runtime code and dependencies are removed from the final application dependency graph.
- [ ] All runtime, API, scoring, session, and UI tests remain green after the old execution path is removed.

## Blocked by

- #14
issues.display_title: Complete harness recovery and replace the framework baseline

issues.title: Harden planner-executor handoffs and budgets for complex Sales tasks
issues.body:
## Context

Blocked follow-up for #17 before running the held-out evaluation.

Two diagnostics of `sales.full_sales_cycle_orchestrator` exposed structural limits in the custom planner-executor harness. In the latest Agent UI run, step 2 successfully advanced and verified the Salesforce opportunity, but all four executor turns contained tool work. The controller then terminated with `executor_turns_per_attempt=4` before the executor could emit a structured `StepOutcome` or the reviewer could run. The run stopped at 0.125 partial credit with no provider, authentication, protocol, or tool failure.

The task also showed that a six-step complex workflow can realistically require about 25 logical model calls before recovery, while the current global limit is 24.

## Goal

Make bounded complex-task execution reliably hand control from executor to reviewer, let discoveries invalidate pending work, ground plans in available capabilities, and retain honest error and budget evidence.

## Requirements

### Executor handoff

- Separate the per-step budget into four tool-capable executor turns plus one reserved outcome call.
- Include `tool_turns_used`, `tool_turns_remaining`, and the reserved-outcome policy in every executor request.
- If the executor has not emitted a valid `StepOutcome` after its fourth tool-capable turn, invoke one finalization call with tools disabled.
- The finalization call must report completed actions, exact evidence with source call IDs, unresolved requirements, and errors.
- Skip the reserved call when a valid outcome was returned earlier.
- Count finalization against the global logical-model-call budget.
- Do not allow the reserved call to request another tool.

### Reviewer plan visibility

- Send the reviewer the current full plan or, at minimum, completed steps plus all remaining steps.
- Let the reviewer return `replan` when newly accepted evidence reveals that pending steps are incomplete or invalid.
- Preserve completed evidence and side-effect records when replacing pending work.
- Keep the reviewer as a separate stateless call to the same model; do not introduce another agent abstraction.

### Tool-grounded planning

- Give planner and replanner calls the declared public tool capabilities; evaluator-only state must remain hidden.
- Require each plan evidence item to identify one or more declared source tools capable of producing it.
- Reject or correct plans that reference undeclared tools or evidence with no declared source.
- Encourage the smallest cohesive plan rather than filling all six available step slots.

### Tool-error normalization

- Convert tool payloads with `success: false` or a non-null top-level `error` into `ToolResult.error`.
- Emit `TOOL_ERROR`, not `TOOL_RESULT`, for those failures while preserving the raw returned payload for diagnosis.
- Ensure error metrics, retry/replan context, and side-effect records distinguish failed reads from successful actions.

### Budgets

- Keep four tool-capable turns per step.
- Reserve one additional no-tools outcome call per saturated step.
- Increase the global logical-model-call limit from 24 to 30.
- Expose the frozen limits in evaluation configuration, traces, and documentation.
- Preserve explicit `budget_exhausted` termination when the global or recovery limits are genuinely reached.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A scripted step that uses all four tool-capable turns receives a fifth no-tools finalization call, emits `StepOutcome`, and reaches the reviewer instead of terminating on the per-step budget.
- [ ] The executor can see accurate remaining-turn metadata on every call.
- [ ] The finalization request exposes no tools and cannot dispatch a side effect.
- [ ] A scripted playbook discovery that adds missing Calendly and Zoom work causes the reviewer to replan because it can compare the discovery with remaining steps.
- [ ] Completed evidence and successful side effects remain immutable across that replan.
- [ ] Planner and replanner requests contain public tool capabilities but no task ID, assertions, expected values, repetition metadata, or raw world state.
- [ ] Plan evidence sources are validated against declared tool names.
- [ ] `{"success": false, "error": "..."}` and `{"error": "..."}` tool responses produce correlated `TOOL_ERROR` events and appear in evaluation error metrics.
- [ ] The frozen global logical-model-call limit is 30 and is included in artifacts.
- [ ] A scripted end-to-end `sales.full_sales_cycle_orchestrator` run through real AutomationBench tools reaches strict official completion within the new limits.
- [ ] Existing blindness, no-duplicate-side-effect, cancellation, retry, replan, UI integration, and evaluation tests continue to pass.
- [ ] Documentation distinguishes tool-capable turns, the reserved outcome call, reviewer calls, and the global logical-call budget.

## Test seam

Use the existing public boundary:

```python
AgentRuntime.run(RuntimeRequest) -> RuntimeOutcome
```

Drive model behavior through the scripted external-model boundary and use real AutomationBench tools/world/scoring. Follow red-green TDD in vertical cycles.

## Non-goals

- Raising the per-step tool budget to six or eight without a forced handoff.
- Adding a new agent framework, persistent reviewer agent, DAG planner, concurrency, or generalized workflow engine.
- Exposing evaluator-owned assertions, expected values, task metadata, or raw world state to any model call.

issues.title: Run the held-out evaluation and document the results
issues.body:
## Parent

- #13

## What to build

Execute the final preregistered Sales evaluation with the completed harness and turn the resulting evidence into the case-study deliverables.

The owner will provide ten held-out Sales task IDs that exclude the fixed development set. Run ten scorable repetitions per task under one frozen GPT-5.6 Sol, harness, prompt, and evaluation-protocol configuration. Regenerate the aggregate reports, preserve an inspectable example run, and document what the experiment demonstrates, where the agent fails, the tradeoffs made for simplicity, and what should be improved next.

The documentation must let a reviewer install, run, inspect, and understand the custom agent and its evaluation without relying on the historical LangGraph prototype.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A preregistered manifest contains ten held-out Sales tasks and excludes sales.update_contact_phone, sales.negative_selection, sales.dependency_chain, and sales.zoom_calendar_conflict.
- [ ] The full evaluation produces ten scorable runs for each selected task under one frozen configuration.
- [ ] Infrastructure-invalid attempts are replaced and do not appear as agent observations in the final aggregate.
- [ ] The final Markdown and JSON reports are regenerated from the persisted artifacts.
- [ ] The report clearly displays per-task results and the overall selected-panel summary without hiding sample size or run-to-run variation.
- [ ] At least one real development-task execution is preserved as a complete example with plan, steps, tool calls, reviews, final response, world changes, and official score.
- [ ] Documentation explains the planner-executor flow, blindness boundary, tool and information flow, context compression, recovery transitions, execution budgets, observability, and termination behavior.
- [ ] Documentation explains the development/held-out protocol, frozen identifiers, ten-by-ten execution, metrics, resumability, and infrastructure-attempt treatment.
- [ ] Documentation reports actual findings, recurring failure modes, efficiency observations, and changes justified by development evaluation.
- [ ] Documentation identifies explicit non-goals, time and complexity tradeoffs, and prioritized improvements for additional time.
- [ ] Setup and run instructions reproduce a single UI execution, a batch evaluation, and offline report generation.
- [ ] The final repository default path uses only the custom framework-free runtime.
- [ ] All automated tests pass and the documented example and report commands complete successfully.

## Blocked by

- #16
- Owner-provided preregistered ten-task held-out manifest

issue.title: Build the framework-free planner–executor agent
issue.body:
## Parent

- #13

## What to build

Build the first complete vertical slice of the custom agent: one AutomationBench Sales task must run from the existing evaluator interface through a framework-free planner-executor runtime, mutate a fresh private world through declared tools, receive planner review, produce a final response, emit an inspectable trace, and receive the official deterministic score.

The slice must establish the blindness boundary by design. The planner and executor receive only the task prompt, public tool specifications, observed tool results, plan state, and execution budgets. The AutomationBench adapter privately owns task identity, assertions, expected values, world state, bound callables, and scoring.

Use GPT-5.6 Sol through the existing Libra-compatible Responses API endpoint with direct provider SDK calls. Keep the current LangGraph runtime temporarily available while this new path is proven.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A framework-neutral AutomationBench adapter creates a fresh private world and exposes only prompt messages, public tool specifications, and an opaque dispatcher to the agent.
- [ ] Tool specifications contain name, description, and JSON input schema generated without LangChain.
- [ ] Tool arguments are validated before dispatch, and the agent cannot access assertions, expected values, or raw world state.
- [ ] The planner creates a structured linear plan with no more than six ordered steps and explicit completion evidence.
- [ ] The executor runs a local ReAct loop for the current step and can execute multiple declared tool calls in one logical model turn.
- [ ] The planner reviews executor evidence and returns structured step_completed, retry_step, replan, or goal_completed decisions.
- [ ] A happy-path multi-step execution reaches goal_completed and returns the planner's user-facing final response.
- [ ] The adapter applies the official AutomationBench scorer only after agent execution and returns partial credit, strict completion, assertion evidence, and final world state outside the agent boundary.
- [ ] Planning, step start, executor turns, tool calls, tool results, reviews, and completion are emitted as ordered correlated runtime events.
- [ ] The new runtime satisfies the existing AgentRuntime request/outcome contract and can be injected into the existing evaluator UI.
- [ ] High-level scripted-model tests exercise the complete runtime seam with real AutomationBench tools and official scoring.
- [ ] One real development task runs end to end against GPT-5.6 Sol through the custom runtime.
- [ ] Existing LangGraph runtime behavior remains available and the repository test suites remain green during this expand step.

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.

issue.title: Build the resumable evaluation and aggregate report workflow
issue.body:
## Parent

- #13

## What to build

Provide one simple offline evaluation workflow that runs the completed custom agent repeatedly and produces both durable run evidence and aggregate results.

The workflow accepts a preregistered Sales task manifest and a frozen evaluation configuration, executes tasks sequentially against fresh worlds, persists every scorable repetition immediately, resumes missing repetitions after interruption, and replaces endpoint-infrastructure attempts that never produced an agent observation.

The same workflow must generate deterministic Markdown and JSON reports grouped by full configuration identity and task. It must remain a command-line workflow with no report server, database, dashboard, or subjective model evaluator.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The evaluation configuration records model, harness version, prompt version, evaluation protocol version, and frozen execution limits.
- [ ] The runner accepts a task manifest and requested repetition count without exposing task, held-out, repetition, assertion, score, or world metadata to the agent.
- [ ] Every repetition creates a fresh AutomationBench world and empty agent conversation state.
- [ ] Runs execute sequentially through the completed custom AgentRuntime.
- [ ] Every scorable run is persisted immediately with configuration identity, task, repetition, run ID, timing, status, termination reason, complete trace, provider retry count, token usage, response, worlds, official score, and assertion evidence.
- [ ] Agent and harness failures remain persisted and satisfy their task-repetition pair.
- [ ] Exhausted transient endpoint failures produce no scorable artifact and are replaced until the requested number of agent observations exists.

issue.title: Harden planner-executor handoffs and budgets for complex Sales tasks
issue.body:
## Context

Blocked follow-up for #17 before running the held-out evaluation.

Two diagnostics of `sales.full_sales_cycle_orchestrator` exposed structural limits in the custom planner-executor harness. In the latest Agent UI run, step 2 successfully advanced and verified the Salesforce opportunity, but all four executor turns contained tool work. The controller then terminated with `executor_turns_per_attempt=4` before the executor could emit a structured `StepOutcome` or the reviewer could run. The run stopped at 0.125 partial credit with no provider, authentication, protocol, or tool failure.

The task also showed that a six-step complex workflow can realistically require about 25 logical model calls before recovery, while the current global limit is 24.

## Goal

Make bounded complex-task execution reliably hand control from executor to reviewer, let discoveries invalidate pending work, ground plans in available capabilities, and retain honest error and budget evidence.

## Requirements

### Executor handoff

- Separate the per-step budget into four tool-capable executor turns plus one reserved outcome call.
- Include `tool_turns_used`, `tool_turns_remaining`, and the reserved-outcome policy in every executor request.
- If the executor has not emitted a valid `StepOutcome` after its fourth tool-capable turn, invoke one finalization call with tools disabled.
- The finalization call must report completed actions, exact evidence with source call IDs, unresolved requirements, and errors.
- Skip the reserved call when a valid outcome was returned earlier.
- Count finalization against the global logical-model-call budget.
- Do not allow the reserved call to request another tool.

### Reviewer plan visibility

- Send the reviewer the current full plan or, at minimum, completed steps plus all remaining steps.
- Let the reviewer return `replan` when newly accepted evidence reveals that pending steps are incomplete or invalid.
- Preserve completed evidence and side-effect records when replacing pending work.
- Keep the reviewer as a separate stateless call to the same model; do not introduce another agent abstraction.

### Tool-grounded planning

- Give planner and replanner calls the declared public tool capabilities; evaluator-only state must remain hidden.
- Require each plan evidence item to identify one or more declared source tools capable of producing it.
- Reject or correct plans that reference undeclared tools or evidence with no declared source.
- Encourage the smallest cohesive plan rather than filling all six available step slots.

### Tool-error normalization

- Convert tool payloads with `success: false` or a non-null top-level `error` into `ToolResult.error`.
- Emit `TOOL_ERROR`, not `TOOL_RESULT`, for those failures while preserving the raw returned payload for diagnosis.
- Ensure error metrics, retry/replan context, and side-effect records distinguish failed reads from successful actions.

### Budgets

- Keep four tool-capable turns per step.
- Reserve one additional no-tools outcome call per saturated step.
- Increase the global logical-model-call limit from 24 to 30.
- Expose the frozen limits in evaluation configuration, traces, and documentation.
- Preserve explicit `budget_exhausted` termination when the global or recovery limits are genuinely reached.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A scripted step that uses all four tool-capable turns receives a fifth no-tools finalization call, emits `StepOutcome`, and reaches the reviewer instead of terminating on the per-step budget.
- [ ] The executor can see accurate remaining-turn metadata on every call.
- [ ] The finalization request exposes no tools and cannot dispatch a side effect.
- [ ] A scripted playbook discovery that adds missing Calendly and Zoom work causes the reviewer to replan because it can compare the discovery with remaining steps.
- [ ] Completed evidence and successful side effects remain immutable across that replan.
- [ ] Planner and replanner requests contain public tool capabilities but no task ID, assertions, expected values, repetition metadata, or raw world state.
- [ ] Plan evidence sources are validated against declared tool names.
- [ ] `{"success": false, "error": "..."}` and `{"error": "..."}` tool responses produce correlated `TOOL_ERROR` events and appear in evaluation error metrics.
- [ ] The frozen global logical-model-call limit is 30 and is included in artifacts.
- [ ] A scripted end-to-end `sales.full_sales_cycle_orchestrator` run through real AutomationBench tools reaches strict official completion within the new limits.
- [ ] Existing blindness, no-duplicate-side-effect, cancellation, retry, replan, UI integration, and evaluation tests continue to pass.
- [ ] Documentation distinguishes tool-capable turns, the reserved outcome call, reviewer calls, and the global logical-call budget.

## Test seam

Use the existing public boundary:

```python
AgentRuntime.run(RuntimeRequest) -> RuntimeOutcome
```

Drive model behavior through the scripted external-model boundary and use real AutomationBench tools/world/scoring. Follow red-green TDD in vertical cycles.

## Non-goals

- Raising the per-step tool budget to six or eight without a forced handoff.
- Adding a new agent framework, persistent reviewer agent, DAG planner, concurrency, or generalized workflow engine.
- Exposing evaluator-owned assertions, expected values, task metadata, or raw world state to any model call.

- #13

## What to build

Complete the custom planner-executor harness by adding its bounded recovery and context behavior, then make it the sole runtime used by the application.

The planner must be able to reject one step attempt, provide corrective feedback, retry it once, and replace an invalid pending plan once while preserving accepted evidence and previous side effects. Every execution must end predictably under the agreed budgets, protocol rules, tool errors, cancellation, or model failures.

Once this behavior is proven through the public runtime seam, remove the exploratory LangChain and LangGraph execution path and dependencies. Preserve existing evaluator UI, durable session, scoring, and trace behavior while extending the trace to explain recovery and terminal states.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A rejected step can be retried once with the prior local transcript and planner feedback available to the executor.
- [ ] A plan can be replaced once while completed steps and accepted evidence remain immutable.
- [ ] Accepted step transcripts are removed from future model-visible context and replaced by structured evidence preserving exact facts, source tool-call IDs, actions, and outcomes.
- [ ] Before replanning, failed attempts become structured records preserving performed actions, side effects that must not be repeated, useful facts, errors, and the invalidation reason.
- [ ] Context is not compressed between ordinary executor turns or before the one permitted retry.
- [ ] Unknown tools, invalid arguments, and tool exceptions are returned as structured correlated errors that the executor can react to.
- [ ] Side-effecting tool calls are never automatically retried by Python.
- [ ] Transient network errors, HTTP 429, and HTTP 5xx receive no more than two provider retries with short backoff.
- [ ] Planner and reviewer schema failures receive one correction attempt and then terminate as model_protocol_error.
- [ ] The runtime enforces no more than six plan steps, four executor model turns per attempt, one step retry, one replan, and 24 logical model calls.
- [ ] Exhausted execution limits terminate as budget_exhausted and remain scorable agent outcomes.
- [ ] Cooperative cancellation produces a stopped outcome at a safe model or completed tool-batch boundary.
- [ ] Retry, replan, protocol correction, cancellation, budget exhaustion, and completion appear as ordered correlated events and durable terminal evidence in the existing UI.
- [ ] High-level scripted tests cover retry, replan, context compression, tool-error recovery, every budget, protocol failure, cancellation, and termination reasons through AgentRuntime.
- [ ] The custom planner-executor becomes the default runtime for the UI and command-line workflow.
- [ ] LangChain and LangGraph runtime code and dependencies are removed from the final application dependency graph.
- [ ] All runtime, API, scoring, session, and UI tests remain green after the old execution path is removed.

  • Required the finalizer to return completed actions, exact evidence/source call IDs, unresolved requirements, and errors. Finalization counts against the shared logical-model-call budget and cannot dispatch tool calls.
  • Raised and centralized the global logical-model-call limit at 30, emitted the frozen limits in the planning trace, and enforced the same limits in evaluation configuration/documentation.
  • Gave planner/replanner calls declared public tool schemas, added per-evidence source-tool declarations, and protocol-corrected/rejected undeclared sources while keeping evaluator-owned state hidden.
  • Gave each reviewer the full current plan plus accepted evidence. Replanning now preserves completed evidence and exact successful side-effect records while replacing only pending work.
  • Normalized real tool payloads with success: false or a non-null top-level error into correlated TOOL_ERROR events while retaining raw payloads and distinguishing failed reads/writes from successful side effects.
  • Updated Agent UI plan rendering for structured evidence while retaining compatibility with historical string evidence.

TDD and verification:

  • Reworked the focused public-seam regression (AgentRuntime.run(RuntimeRequest) -> RuntimeOutcome) red-first around the saturated-step handoff, then made it green with the fifth no-tools finalizer and reviewer handoff.
  • mock-agent: 21 tests passed.
  • Agent UI integration/browser suite: 27 tests passed.
  • A compact scripted sales.full_sales_cycle_orchestrator run through real AutomationBench tools/world/scoring completed in 5 logical calls with partial_credit=1.0 and task_completed_correctly=1.0.

Implemented the complete recovery harness and replaced the framework baseline.

Implementation details:

  • Added one run-wide rejected-step retry with intact local transcript, prior outcome, planner feedback, and no implicit tool replay.
  • Added one bounded replan with immutable accepted evidence and structured failed-attempt records (useful facts, source call IDs, performed actions, successful calls not to repeat, tool errors, and invalidation reason).
  • Added structured unknown-tool, invalid-argument, and tool-exception recovery through the public runtime seam.
  • Added explicit provider retry policy for network/429/5xx failures (two retries maximum, short backoff, SDK hidden retries disabled).
  • Added one planner/reviewer protocol correction followed by stable model_protocol_error termination.
  • Enforced six plan steps, four executor turns per attempt, one run-wide step retry, one replan, and 24 logical model calls. Exhaustion returns a scorable budget_exhausted outcome.
  • Added safe cooperative cancellation at model and completed tool-batch boundaries.
  • Added correlated retry, replan, provider/protocol, model-error, budget, cancellation, and completion events plus durable session termination reasons and UI rendering.
  • Made the custom planner-executor the sole UI and CLI runtime.
  • Removed the LangChain/LangGraph runtime, tests, dependencies, lockfile entries, and installed packages.

Implemented issue #19 in local commit 05a09bf (feat(agent): harden planner executor handoffs). The commit has not been pushed.

Implementation details:

  • Kept four tool-capable executor turns and added one reserved, tools-disabled finalization call for saturated steps. Every executor request now carries used/remaining turn metadata and the reserved-outcome policy; early valid outcomes skip the reserved call.
  • Required the finalizer to return completed actions, exact evidence/source call IDs, unresolved requirements, and errors. Finalization counts against the shared logical-model-call budget and cannot dispatch tool calls.
  • Raised and centralized the global logical-model-call limit at 30, emitted the frozen limits in the planning trace, and enforced the same limits in evaluation configuration/documentation.
  • Gave planner/replanner calls declared public tool schemas, added per-evidence source-tool declarations, and protocol-corrected/rejected undeclared sources while keeping evaluator-owned state hidden.
  • Gave each reviewer the full current plan plus accepted evidence. Replanning now preserves completed evidence and exact successful side-effect records while replacing only pending work.
  • Normalized real tool payloads with success: false or a non-null top-level error into correlated TOOL_ERROR events while retaining raw payloads and distinguishing failed reads/writes from successful side effects.
  • Updated Agent UI plan rendering for structured evidence while retaining compatibility with historical string evidence.

TDD and verification:

  • Reworked the focused public-seam regression (AgentRuntime.run(RuntimeRequest) -> RuntimeOutcome) red-first around the saturated-step handoff, then made it green with the fifth no-tools finalizer and reviewer handoff.
  • mock-agent: 21 tests passed.
  • Agent UI integration/browser suite: 27 tests passed.
  • A compact scripted sales.full_sales_cycle_orchestrator run through real AutomationBench tools/world/scoring completed in 5 logical calls with partial_credit=1.0 and task_completed_correctly=1.0.

    70	            "instructions": request.instructions,

    72	            "store": False,
    73	        }
    74	        if request.tools:
    75	            values["tools"] = [
    76	                {
    77	                    "type": "function",
    78	                    "name": tool.name,
    79	                    "description": tool.description,
    80	                    "parameters": tool.input_schema,
    81	                    "strict": False,
    82	                }
    83	                for tool in request.tools
    84	            ]
    85	        retries = []
    86	        for attempt in range(3):
    87	            try:
    88	                if request.response_model is None:
    89	                    response = await client.responses.create(**values)
    90	                else:
    91	                    response = await client.responses.parse(
    92	                        **values,
    93	                        text_format=request.response_model,
    94	                    )
    95	                break
    96	            except ValidationError as error:
    97	                return ModelReply(
    98	                    content=None,
    99	                    metadata={
   100	                        "structured_output_error": error.errors(
   101	                            include_url=False, include_input=False
   102	                        )
   103	                    },
   104	                )
   105	            except Exception as error:
   106	                if attempt == 2 or not self._is_transient(error):
   107	                    raise ProviderFailure(
   108	                        error, retries, transient=self._is_transient(error)
   109	                    ) from error
   110	                retries.append(
   111	                    {
   112	                        "retry": attempt + 1,
   113	                        "max_retries": 2,
   114	                        "error_type": type(error).__name__,
   115	                        "status_code": self._status_code(error),
   116	                    }
   117	                )
   118	                await asyncio.sleep(self._retry_delays[attempt])
   119	        calls = []
   120	        for item in response.output:
   121	            if getattr(item, "type", None) != "function_call":
   122	                continue
   123	            calls.append(
   124	                ToolCall(
   125	                    id=item.call_id,
   126	                    name=item.name,
   127	                    arguments=json.loads(item.arguments),
   128	                )
   129	            )
   130	        parsed = getattr(response, "output_parsed", None)
   131	        content = (
   132	            parsed.model_dump(mode="json")
   133	            if parsed is not None
   134	            else getattr(response, "output_text", None)
   135	        )
   136	        usage = getattr(response, "usage", None)
   137	        usage_values = {
   138	            key: int(getattr(usage, key, 0) or 0)
   139	            for key in ("input_tokens", "output_tokens", "total_tokens")
   140	        }
   141	        return ModelReply(
   142	            content=content,
   143	            tool_calls=tuple(calls),
   144	            usage=usage_values,
   145	            metadata={
   146	                "response_id": response.id,
   147	                "status": response.status,
   148	                "provider_retries": retries,
   149	            },
   150	        )
   151	
   152	    @classmethod
   153	    def _is_transient(cls, error: Exception) -> bool:
   154	        status_code = cls._status_code(error)
   155	        return (
   156	            isinstance(error, (APIConnectionError, ConnectionError, TimeoutError))
   157	            or status_code == 429
   158	            or (status_code is not None and 500 <= status_code < 600)
   159	        )
   160	
   161	    @staticmethod
   162	    def _status_code(error: Exception) -> int | None:
   163	        status_code = getattr(error, "status_code", None)
   164	        if status_code is None:
   165	            status_code = getattr(getattr(error, "response", None), "status_code", None)
   166	        return int(status_code) if status_code is not None else None
   167	
   168	    @staticmethod
   169	    def _input_item(item: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
   170	        content = item.get("content")
   171	        if not isinstance(content, str):
   172	            content = json.dumps(content, ensure_ascii=True, default=str)
   173	        return {"type": "message", "role": item["role"], "content": content}
   174	
   175	    @staticmethod
   176	    def _client_from_environment() -> AsyncOpenAI:
   177	        base_url = os.environ.get("LIBRA_BASE_URL")
   178	        if not base_url:
   179	            raise RuntimeError("Set LIBRA_BASE_URL before running the planner-executor")
   180	        api_key = os.environ.get("LIBRA_INTERVIEW_API_KEY")
   181	        if not api_key:
   182	            raise RuntimeError(
   183	                "Set LIBRA_INTERVIEW_API_KEY before running the planner-executor"
   184	            )
   185	        return AsyncOpenAI(api_key=api_key, base_url=base_url, max_retries=0)

    31	class ToolDispatcher:
    32	    def __init__(
    33	        self,
    34	        tools: dict[str, Any],
    35	        validators: dict[str, type[BaseModel]],
    36	        world: WorldState,
    37	    ):
    38	        self._tools = tools
    39	        self._validators = validators
    40	        self._world = world
    41	
    42	    async def dispatch(self, name: str, arguments: dict[str, Any]) -> ToolResult:
    43	        if name not in self._tools:
    44	            return ToolResult(
    45	                error={"type": "unknown_tool", "message": f"Unknown tool: {name}"}
    46	            )
    47	        try:
    48	            values = self._validators[name].model_validate(arguments).model_dump()
    49	        except ValidationError as error:
    50	            details = [
    51	                {
    52	                    "location": list(item["loc"]),
    53	                    "message": item["msg"],
    54	                    "code": item["type"],
    55	                }
    56	                for item in error.errors(include_url=False, include_input=False)
    57	            ]
    58	            return ToolResult(
    59	                error={
    60	                    "type": "invalid_arguments",
    61	                    "message": "Tool arguments failed validation.",
    62	                    "details": details,
    63	                }
    64	            )
    65	        try:
    66	            result = self._tools[name](self._world, **values)
    67	            if isinstance(result, str):
    68	                try:
    69	                    result = json.loads(result)
    70	                except json.JSONDecodeError:
    71	                    pass
    72	            if isinstance(result, dict) and (
    73	                result.get("success") is False
    74	                or ("error" in result and result["error"] is not None)
    75	            ):
    76	                reported_error = result.get("error")
    77	                return ToolResult(
    78	                    value=result,
    79	                    error={
    80	                        "type": "tool_reported_error",
    81	                        "message": (
    82	                            str(reported_error)
    83	                            if reported_error is not None
    84	                            else "Tool returned success=false."
    85	                        ),
    86	                        "reported_error": reported_error,
    87	                    },
    88	                )
    89	            return ToolResult(value=result)
    90	        except Exception as error:
    91	            return ToolResult(
    92	                error={
    93	                    "type": "tool_exception",
    94	                    "message": f"{type(error).__name__}: {error}",
    95	                }
    96	            )

   121	class AutomationBenchAdapter:
   122	    def __init__(self, catalog: TaskCatalog | None = None):
   123	        self._catalog = catalog or TaskCatalog.from_sales_dataset()
   124	        self._registry = {tool.__name__: tool for tool in ALL_TOOLS}
   125	
   126	    def open(self, task_id: str) -> AutomationBenchSession:
   127	        definition = self._catalog.get_task(task_id)
   128	        world = WorldState(**copy.deepcopy(definition.info["initial_state"]))
   129	        missing = set(definition.summary.tools) - self._registry.keys()
   130	        if missing:
   131	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown task tools: {sorted(missing)}")
   132	        functions = {name: self._registry[name] for name in definition.summary.tools}
   133	        validators = {name: _arguments_model(functions[name]) for name in functions}
   134	        specs = tuple(
   135	            ToolSpec(
   136	                name=name,
   137	                description=functions[name].__doc__ or name,
   138	                input_schema=validators[name].model_json_schema(),
   139	                side_effect=TOOL_METADATA.get(functions[name], {}).get("type") == "write",
   140	            )
   141	            for name in definition.summary.tools
   142	        )
   143	        task = AgentTask(
   144	            prompt=definition.summary.prompt,
   145	            tools=specs,
   146	            dispatcher=ToolDispatcher(functions, validators, world),
   147	        )
   148	        return AutomationBenchSession(definition, world, task)
   149	
   150	
   151	def _arguments_model(function: Any) -> type[BaseModel]:
   152	    hints = get_type_hints(function)
   153	    fields = {}
   154	    for name, parameter in inspect.signature(function).parameters.items():
   155	        if name == "world":
   156	            continue
   157	        default = (
   158	            ... if parameter.default is inspect.Parameter.empty else parameter.default
   159	        )
   160	        fields[name] = (hints.get(name, Any), default)
   161	    return create_model(f"{function.__name__}_arguments", **fields)
   162

   164	def score_world(task: dict[str, Any], world: WorldState) -> dict[str, Any]:
   165	    state = {
   166	        "info": task["info"],
   167	        "initial_state": copy.deepcopy(task["info"]["initial_state"]),
   168	        "world": world,
   169	    }
   170	    return {
   171	        "partial_credit": partial_credit(state),
   172	        "task_completed_correctly": task_completed_correctly(state),
   173	        "assertions": state.get("_assertion_results", []),
   174	    }

   267	function reducePlanEvents(events) {
   268	  const plans = [];
   269	  const reviews = new Map();
   270	  let activeStep = null;
   271	
   272	  const currentPlan = () => plans.at(-1);
   273	  const findStep = (stepId) => {
   274	    for (const plan of [...plans].reverse()) {
   275	      const step = plan.steps.find((item) => item.id === stepId);
   276	      if (step) return step;
   277	    }
   278	    return null;
   279	  };
   280	  const supersedePending = (plan) => {
   281	    if (!plan) return;
   282	    for (const step of plan.steps) {
   283	      if (step.state === "pending") step.state = "superseded";
   284	    }
   285	  };
   286	  const failActive = () => {
   287	    if (activeStep && ["active", "retrying"].includes(activeStep.state)) {
   288	      activeStep.state = "failed";
   289	    }
   290	    activeStep = null;
   291	  };
   292	
   293	  for (const event of [...events].sort((left, right) => left.sequence - right.sequence)) {
   294	    if (event.kind === "plan_created") {
   295	      const previous = currentPlan();
   296	      if (previous) {
   297	        failActive();
   298	        supersedePending(previous);
   299	        previous.current = false;
   300	      }
   301	      const content = event.content || {};
   302	      plans.push({
   303	        id: event.correlation_id,
   304	        goal: content.goal || "Goal unavailable",
   305	        current: true,
   306	        steps: (content.steps || []).map((step) => ({
   307	          id: step.id,
   308	          objective: step.objective,
   309	          requiredEvidence: Array.isArray(step.required_evidence)
   310	            ? step.required_evidence.map((item) =>
   311	                typeof item === "string" ? item : item.requirement,
   312	              )
   313	            : [],
   314	          state: "pending",
   315	        })),
   316	      });
   317	    } else if (event.kind === "step_started") {
   318	      const step = findStep(event.content?.id || event.correlation_id);
   319	      if (step) {
   320	        failActive();
   321	        step.state = "active";
   322	        activeStep = step;
   323	      }
   324	    } else if (event.kind === "review") {
   325	      const step = findStep(event.parent_id);
   326	      reviews.set(event.correlation_id, step);
   327	      const decision = event.content?.decision;
   328	      if (step && ["step_completed", "goal_completed"].includes(decision)) {
   329	        step.state = "completed";
   330	        if (activeStep === step) activeStep = null;
   331	      } else if (step && decision === "retry_step") {
   332	        step.state = "retrying";
   333	        activeStep = step;
   334	      } else if (step && decision === "replan") {
   335	        step.state = "failed";
   336	        if (activeStep === step) activeStep = null;
   337	      }
   338	    } else if (event.kind === "step_retry") {
   339	      const step = findStep(event.correlation_id);
   340	      if (step) {
   341	        step.state = "retrying";
   342	        activeStep = step;
   343	      }
   344	    } else if (event.kind === "replan") {
   345	      const failed = reviews.get(event.parent_id);
   346	      if (failed && failed.state !== "completed") failed.state = "failed";
   347	      failActive();
   348	      supersedePending(currentPlan());
   349	    } else if (["cancellation", "budget_exhausted", "protocol_error", "model_error"].includes(event.kind)) {
   350	      failActive();
   351	    } else if (event.kind === "completion") {
   352	      if (event.content?.status === "completed") {
   353	        if (activeStep) activeStep.state = "completed";
   354	        activeStep = null;
   355	        supersedePending(currentPlan());
   356	      } else {
   357	        failActive();
   358	      }
   359	    }
   360	  }
   361	  return { plans };
   362	}

   378	    elements.planGoal.textContent = "";

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import datetime, timezone
import inspect
from time import monotonic
from typing import Any, Literal
from uuid import uuid4

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, model_validator

from mock_agent.adapter import AutomationBenchAdapter
from mock_agent.contract import (
    CancellationSignal,
    EventKind,
    EventSink,
    ExitStatus,
    RuntimeEvent,
    RuntimeOutcome,
    RuntimeRequest,
)
from mock_agent.model import ModelClient, ModelReply, ModelRequest


PLANNER_PROMPT = """planner/v1
Create a linear plan of at most six steps. Each step needs a stable ID, one objective,
and explicit observable completion evidence. Use only the supplied task and tools.
"""
EXECUTOR_PROMPT = """executor/v1
Execute only the current step. Use declared tools for evidence or actions. You may call
multiple tools in one turn. When done, return exact evidence with source call IDs.
"""
REVIEWER_PROMPT = """reviewer/v1
Review the step outcome against its required evidence. Decide step_completed,
retry_step, replan, or goal_completed. Only goal_completed may return the final response.
"""


class PlanStep(BaseModel):
    id: str
    objective: str
    required_evidence: list[str] = Field(min_length=1)


class Plan(BaseModel):
    goal: str
    steps: list[PlanStep] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=6)

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def unique_step_ids(self):
        if len({step.id for step in self.steps}) != len(self.steps):
            raise ValueError("Plan step IDs must be unique")
        return self


class Evidence(BaseModel):
    fact: str
    source_call_id: str


class StepOutcome(BaseModel):
    summary: str
    evidence: list[Evidence]
    actions: list[str]
    errors: list[str]


class Review(BaseModel):
    decision: Literal["step_completed", "retry_step", "replan", "goal_completed"]
    feedback: str | None = None
    final_response: str | None = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def completed_goal_has_response(self):
        if self.decision == "goal_completed" and not self.final_response:
            raise ValueError("goal_completed requires final_response")
        return self


class PlannerExecutorRuntime:
    def __init__(
        self,
        *,
        model_client: ModelClient,
        adapter: AutomationBenchAdapter | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        self._model = model_client
        self._adapter = adapter or AutomationBenchAdapter()

    async def run(
        self,
        request: RuntimeRequest,
        *,
        event_sink: EventSink | None = None,
        cancellation: CancellationSignal | None = None,
    ) -> RuntimeOutcome:
        cancellation = cancellation or CancellationSignal()
        session = self._adapter.open(request.task_id)
        task = session.agent_task
        run_id = str(uuid4())
        events: list[RuntimeEvent] = []
        usage = {"input_tokens": 0, "output_tokens": 0, "total_tokens": 0}
        final_response = None

        async def emit(kind: EventKind, correlation_id: str, **values: Any) -> None:
            event = RuntimeEvent(
                sequence=len(events) + 1,
                kind=kind,
                timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
                run_id=run_id,
                correlation_id=correlation_id,
                **values,
            )
            events.append(event)
            if event_sink is not None:
                result = event_sink(event)
                if inspect.isawaitable(result):
                    await result

        async def ask(model_request: ModelRequest) -> tuple[ModelReply, float]:
            started = monotonic()
            reply = await self._model.respond(model_request)
            for key in usage:
                usage[key] += int(reply.usage.get(key, 0))
            return reply, (monotonic() - started) * 1000

        prompt = tuple(
            {"role": item.role, "content": item.content} for item in task.prompt
        )
        try:
            await emit(EventKind.PLANNING, run_id)
            reply, duration = await ask(
                ModelRequest(
                    role="planner",
                    model_name=request.model_name,
                    instructions=PLANNER_PROMPT,
                    input=prompt,
                    response_model=Plan,
                )
            )
            plan = Plan.model_validate(reply.content)
            plan_id = str(uuid4())
            await emit(
                EventKind.PLAN_CREATED,
                plan_id,
                parent_id=run_id,
                content=plan.model_dump(),
                usage=reply.usage or None,
                duration_ms=duration,
            )
            accepted: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

            for step in plan.steps:
                if cancellation.is_cancelled:
                    return await self._finish(
                        session,
                        request,
                        run_id,
                        events,
                        usage,
                        final_response,
                        ExitStatus.STOPPED,
                        emit,
                    )
                await emit(
                    EventKind.STEP_STARTED,
                    step.id,
                    parent_id=plan_id,
                    content=step.model_dump(),
                )
                transcript: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
                outcome = None
                for _ in range(4):
                    executor_input = prompt + (
                        {
                            "role": "user",
                            "content": {
                                "goal": plan.goal,
                                "current_step": step.model_dump(),
                                "accepted_evidence": accepted,
                                "local_transcript": transcript,
                            },
                        },
                    )
                    turn, duration = await ask(
                        ModelRequest(
                            role="executor",
                            model_name=request.model_name,
                            instructions=EXECUTOR_PROMPT,
                            input=executor_input,
                            tools=task.tools,
                            response_model=StepOutcome,
                        )
                    )
                    turn_id = str(uuid4())
                    await emit(
                        EventKind.EXECUTOR_TURN,
                        turn_id,
                        parent_id=step.id,
                        content=turn.content,
                        usage=turn.usage or None,
                        duration_ms=duration,
                        metadata=turn.metadata,
                    )
                    if not turn.tool_calls:
                        outcome = StepOutcome.model_validate(turn.content)
                        break
                    for call in turn.tool_calls:
                        await emit(
                            EventKind.TOOL_CALL,
                            call.id,
                            parent_id=turn_id,
                            name=call.name,
                            arguments=call.arguments,
                        )
                    for call in turn.tool_calls:
                        started = monotonic()
                        result = await task.dispatcher.dispatch(
                            call.name, call.arguments
                        )
                        duration = (monotonic() - started) * 1000
                        transcript.append(
                            {
                                "tool_call_id": call.id,
                                "name": call.name,
                                "arguments": call.arguments,
                                "result": result.value,
                                "error": result.error,
                            }
                        )
                        await emit(
                            EventKind.TOOL_ERROR
                            if result.error
                            else EventKind.TOOL_RESULT,
                            call.id,
                            name=call.name,
                            result=result.value,
                            error=result.error,
                            duration_ms=duration,
                        )
                if outcome is None:
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"Executor turn limit reached for step {step.id}"
                    )

                review_reply, duration = await ask(
                    ModelRequest(
                        role="reviewer",
                        model_name=request.model_name,
                        instructions=REVIEWER_PROMPT,
                        input=prompt
                        + (
                            {
                                "role": "user",
                                "content": {
                                    "goal": plan.goal,
                                    "step": step.model_dump(),
                                    "outcome": outcome.model_dump(),
                                    "accepted_evidence": accepted,
                                },
                            },
                        ),
                        response_model=Review,
                    )
                )
                review = Review.model_validate(review_reply.content)
                review_id = str(uuid4())
                await emit(
                    EventKind.REVIEW,
                    review_id,
                    parent_id=step.id,
                    content=review.model_dump(),
                    usage=review_reply.usage or None,
                    duration_ms=duration,
                )
                if review.decision == "goal_completed":
                    final_response = review.final_response
                    break
                if review.decision != "step_completed":
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"Unsupported review decision in first slice: {review.decision}"
                    )
                accepted.append(
                    {
                        "step_id": step.id,
                        "summary": outcome.summary,
                        "evidence": [item.model_dump() for item in outcome.evidence],
                        "actions": outcome.actions,
                    }
                )

            if final_response is None:
                raise RuntimeError("Planner did not declare goal_completed")
            return await self._finish(
                session,
                request,
                run_id,
                events,
                usage,
                final_response,
                ExitStatus.COMPLETED,
                emit,
            )
        except Exception as error:
            return await self._finish(
                session,
                request,
                run_id,
                events,
                usage,
                final_response,
                ExitStatus.FAILED,
                emit,
                f"{type(error).__name__}: {error}",
            )

    @staticmethod
    async def _finish(
        session,
        request,
        run_id,
        events,
        usage,
        final_response,
        status,
        emit,
        terminal_error=None,
    ):
        await emit(
            EventKind.COMPLETION,
            run_id,
            content={"status": status.value},
            error=terminal_error,
        )
        score, world = session.evaluate()
        return RuntimeOutcome(
            status=status,
            task_id=request.task_id,
            run_id=run_id,
            events=tuple(events),
            final_response=final_response,
            world_state=world,
            score=score,
            usage=usage,
            terminal_error=terminal_error,
        )

payload.message: The history supports a specific conclusion: the extra machinery was not gratuitous at the time—it fixed real failures. Issue #15 added bounded recovery after unsupported retry/replan paths; issue #19 added the fifth “finalization” call because the executor could consume all four turns on tools and die before handing off. The refactor therefore must eliminate those handoff boundaries structurally, not merely lower their budgets or split the 795-line file.

The refactor therefore must eliminate those handoff boundaries structurally, not merely lower their budgets or split the 795-line file.

+function sessionRuntime(session) {
+  if (session.runtime?.label && session.runtime?.version) return session.runtime;
+  const version = session.agent?.agent_version || "Unknown";

+    id: session.agent?.runtime_id || "legacy",

+  };
+}
+
+function reducePlanEvents(events) {
+  const plans = [];
+  const reviews = new Map();
+  let activeStep = null;
+
+  const currentPlan = () => plans.at(-1);
+  const findStep = (stepId) => {
+    for (const plan of [...plans].reverse()) {
+      const step = plan.steps.find((item) => item.id === stepId);
+      if (step) return step;
+    }
+    return null;
+  };
+  const supersedePending = (plan) => {
+    if (!plan) return;
+    for (const step of plan.steps) {
+      if (step.state === "pending") step.state = "superseded";
+    }
+  };
+  const failActive = () => {
+    if (activeStep && ["active", "retrying"].includes(activeStep.state)) {
+      activeStep.state = "failed";
+    }
+    activeStep = null;
+  };
+
+  for (const event of [...events].sort((left, right) => left.sequence - right.sequence)) {
+    if (event.kind === "plan_created") {
+      const previous = currentPlan();
+      if (previous) {
+        failActive();
+        supersedePending(previous);
+        previous.current = false;
+      }
+      const content = event.content || {};
+      plans.push({
+        id: event.correlation_id,
+        goal: content.goal || "Goal unavailable",
+        current: true,
+        steps: (content.steps || []).map((step) => ({
+          id: step.id,
+          objective: step.objective,
+          requiredEvidence: Array.isArray(step.required_evidence) ? step.required_evidence : [],
+          state: "pending",
+        })),
+      });
+    } else if (event.kind === "step_started") {
+      const step = findStep(event.content?.id || event.correlation_id);
+      if (step) {
+        failActive();
+        step.state = "active";
+        activeStep = step;
+      }
+    } else if (event.kind === "review") {
+      const step = findStep(event.parent_id);
+      reviews.set(event.correlation_id, step);
+      const decision = event.content?.decision;
+      if (step && ["step_completed", "goal_completed"].includes(decision)) {
+        step.state = "completed";
+        if (activeStep === step) activeStep = null;
+      } else if (step && decision === "retry_step") {
+        step.state = "retrying";
+        activeStep = step;
+      } else if (step && decision === "replan") {
+        step.state = "failed";
+        if (activeStep === step) activeStep = null;
+      }
+    } else if (event.kind === "step_retry") {
+      const step = findStep(event.correlation_id);
+      if (step) {
+        step.state = "retrying";
+        activeStep = step;
+      }
+    } else if (event.kind === "replan") {
+      const failed = reviews.get(event.parent_id);
+      if (failed && failed.state !== "completed") failed.state = "failed";
+      failActive();
+      supersedePending(currentPlan());
+    } else if (["cancellation", "budget_exhausted", "protocol_error", "model_error"].includes(event.kind)) {
+      failActive();
+    } else if (event.kind === "completion") {
+      if (event.content?.status === "completed") {
+        if (activeStep) activeStep.state = "completed";
+        activeStep = null;
+        supersedePending(currentPlan());
+      } else {
+        failActive();
+      }
+    }
+  }
+  return { plans };
+}
+
+function renderPlan(session) {
+  const reduced = reducePlanEvents(session.events || []);

+  elements.planGoal.hidden = !hasPlan;
+  elements.planGenerations.hidden = !hasPlan;
+  elements.planGenerations.replaceChildren();
+  if (!hasPlan) {
+    elements.planSummary.textContent = runtime.label;
+    elements.planGoal.textContent = "";
+    return;
+  }
+
+  const latestPlan = reduced.plans.at(-1);
+  const steps = reduced.plans.flatMap((plan) => plan.steps);
+  const completed = steps.filter((step) => step.state === "completed").length;
+  elements.planGoal.textContent = latestPlan.goal;
+  elements.planSummary.textContent = `${completed} of ${steps.length} completed`;
+
+  reduced.plans.forEach((plan, planIndex) => {
+    const generation = document.createElement("section");
